title: eremophila micrantha
intro: Eremophila micrantha, also known as the Small-flowered Emu Bush, is a low-growing, shrubby perennial plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Eremophila micrantha include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
